column14: Good mmnt overall, one edge panel with issues due to shallow depth. Lowered float onto ice, well still isolated. Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Fill; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Moderate Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: Significant alteration of channel bed around gauge and bridge, some spots main flow is on the opposite side. At the gauge mmuch more flow by the orifice on the right bank than previous
column14: High quality wade, no issues. The orifice line of the accubar is buried in rocks and cobbles, I tried redploy but the flow is quite deep and fast along the bank now, Well seems to be functioning, if HG data is way out will start using HG2. Lots of surge along the bank now. Unsure of well connectivity, flushed intakes, seems slow to drain on lower intake, potentially covered in rocks and cobbles as well. See if it recovers after flush. Left to both settle while measuring, will come back to shoot second water level and reset again. Well seems to have recovered, will use well data until I can instal a new secure orifice line, suspect HG will not settle and have significant surge.  Mid-section

column12: Measurement condition : Fill; Partly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Very Light Wind Blowing Cross Stream
column13: debris (large trees) scattered upstream and downstream in channel. Seems that highwater caused channel deposition at gauge, large shift suggest lots of rocks and sediment has filled the channel at the section of the gauge, still to high to fully assess.
column14: Good quality M9 ADCP mmnt from the bridge. Shallow and fast section with angular flow, could not measure 50% and some directional bias observed. ADCP by Moving Boat

column12: Measurement condition : Debris; Cloudy , Moderate Rain , Light Wind Blowing Cross Stream
column13: Still the odd large tree flowing down the river, far side of river into trees and vegetation. Likely significant scour on site after water recedes.
column14: Issues with bottom tracking, moving bed so VTG used. Some fairly large issues with mmnt, should be reviewed, but believed to be useable. Right after shooting water level a giant tree with root wad attached dragged over the orifice line. Unsure of first water level, shot second water level again, no confidence in the first water level, likely just need to remove it and align the stage data with the second water level and adjust the MGH using HG2 logged values. ADCP by Moving Boat
